reviews.review_msg: Awesome profs, very helpful. My favourite COMN prof)
one of the best profs I ever had....thank you.

I took the distance ed version so he's just the course chair. Assignments were fair and there was no midterm. Final tested our broad understanding not specifics. Overall a good class, would recommend!

i found the topics very interesting. He sets lectures up well, however they aren't mandatory as lectures are recorded. I liked that he was very interested in teaching the course. Maybe a little keen with all the emails, but it was always a nice break in lecture when he would show a bunch of videos related to what he was talking about.
His lectures are useless. since cmns 130 is a writing course. You'll be fine as long as you do okay on those essays.

Avoid him at all cost. Strangely-worded papers and lengthy emails leave you in the dark. He TRIES to be helpful, but it just doesn't work out. Lots and lots of reading. Make sure your writing skill is solid when taking this course.
Can wander off, but generally interesting (and certainly interested in what he does!)
He is totally off topic. Makes irrelevant topics during class. I totally lost interested in the course because of his lack of enthusiasm

This was the worst communications class i had ever taken. Ian is a nice guy, but the most interesting part of the lecture is seeing which crazy sweater he will wear next. He tends to ramble and get sidetracked with stories that bear no relevance very easily. The course is not hard, just be sure to attend tutorials and complete all assignments.
